tldr: New Relic is a hosted observability platform with hybrid usage and user pricing and OpenTelemetry support, while Apache SkyWalking is an Apache-licensed self-hosted APM. The move replaces a commit-tier SaaS bill with operational ownership of OAP and storage.
why_switch: Escape New Relic commit-tier minimum spend
Strong topology and tracing for microservices
what_to_export: Custom NRQL queries you depend on
export_method: Use the New Relic API and exports to capture dashboards, alert policies, and entity metadata as JSON. Document any NRQL queries that drive critical alerts or reports so they can be reimplemented as SkyWalking queries or external dashboards. Since New Relic supports OpenTelemetry ingestion, map which services already emit OTel data, those will be the easiest to repoint. For services using New Relic agents, plan a swap to SkyWalking agents or an OTel collector. Keep New Relic active during the migration so you can compare data and not lose history.
import_method: Deploy Apache SkyWalking OAP with a supported storage backend such as Elasticsearch. For services already emitting OpenTelemetry, point the OTel exporter at a collector feeding SkyWalking. For services using New Relic agents, replace them with SkyWalking native agents or OTel auto-instrumentation. Recreate priority dashboards and alert rules in SkyWalking, wiring notifications to your existing channels via SkyWalking's alerting hooks. Validate service topology, traces, and key alerts on a pilot service before rolling out broadly. Decommission New Relic agents and downgrade the contract only after parity is confirmed.
gotchas: Commit-tier pricing means you pay through the contract period regardless of usage
NRQL queries do not translate directly, expect to rebuild reporting
You take on operating OAP and storage, including upgrades and scaling
Historical New Relic data should be considered non-portable, plan overlap
Synthetic checks need a separate solution, SkyWalking is not a synthetics platform
Alerting integrations may need a thin layer between SkyWalking and existing channels

email.subject: Migrating APM from New Relic to Apache SkyWalking over the next 14 days
email.body:
Hi team,

We are moving APM and tracing from New Relic to self-hosted Apache SkyWalking. The drivers are predictable cost, full data ownership, and avoiding commit-tier lock-in. The plan is staged, not a flip.

Plan:
- Days 1-3: export dashboards, alerts, and entity metadata from New Relic and document key NRQL queries.
- Days 4-7: stand up SkyWalking OAP with storage and onboard one OTel-emitting service end to end.
- Days 8-11: replace New Relic agents on a pilot tier of services, validate topology and traces, and wire critical alerts into our existing notification channels.
- Days 12-14: review parity, plan the agent rollout to remaining services, and align on contract timing.

New Relic will stay live during the rollout so we can compare and avoid losing visibility.

Thanks,
Your customer
